# Provenance of `creator-sbom.spdx.json`

`creator-sbom.spdx.json` is a genuine **syft**-generated SPDX 2.3 SBOM slice
used as the baseline test fixture for `SDLC-CTRL-0004/sbom.rego` and
`sbom-facts.jq`. It was produced by `syft-v1.42.3` (`docker buildx ... --sbom`),
so its per-package fields follow syft's conventions -- in particular the real
license lives in `licenseDeclared` while `licenseConcluded` stays `NOASSERTION`.
Every package entry is copied verbatim from a real cyber-dojo build; no field
values were invented or edited. The file is only a *subset* of the original SBOM
(a curated selection of real packages), kept small so it is readable in a repo.

## Where it came from

It is the `sbom` attestation attachment from a real `creator-ci` build in the
cyber-dojo Kosli org:

- flow: `creator-ci`
- artifact fingerprint: `73bd6891f717f49667ce1d3509e556eafea5034688df97393b568a1539bb7818`
- generated by: `syft-v1.42.3` (via `docker buildx ... --sbom`, buildkit-v0.30.0)
- original SBOM: SPDX-2.3, 194 packages, 908 relationships (~975 KB)

Kosli GET requests are read-only and accept any throwaway token, so no real
credentials are needed to reproduce this.

### 1. Find the artifact fingerprint

```sh
kosli list artifacts --flow creator-ci --org cyber-dojo \
  --api-token throwaway-ro --output json | jq -r '.[0].fingerprint'
```

### 2. Get the attestation and its evidence-vault URL

```sh
kosli get attestation sbom \
  --flow creator-ci \
  --fingerprint 73bd6891f717f49667ce1d3509e556eafea5034688df97393b568a1539bb7818 \
  --org cyber-dojo --api-token throwaway-ro --output json \
  | jq -r '(if type=="array" then .[0] else . end)._links.evidence.href'
```

### 3. Download the SBOM attachment from the evidence vault

```sh
curl --fail-with-body -H "Authorization: Bearer throwaway-ro" \
  "<evidence.href from step 2>" > full-sbom.spdx.json
```

The endpoint returns the attached `sbom.spdx.json` verbatim
(`content-disposition: inline; filename="sbom.spdx.json"`).

## How it was trimmed down

A `jq` selection keeps a representative set of real packages plus the SPDX
document metadata, then keeps only the relationships whose endpoints are both in
the retained set (so the `DESCRIBES` edge that marks the document-root package
survives). The 20 selected names match 22 real package entries because a few
names appear in more than one package manager.

```sh
jq '
  ["sbom",".ruby-rundeps","minitest-ci","ruby","selenium-manager","bash","gmp",
   "dmesg","mount","abbrev","addressable","ansi","libuuid","hexdump","skalibs-libs",
   "agetty","alpine-keys","json","libncursesw","zlib"] as $names
  | ([ .packages[] | select(.name as $n | $names | index($n)) ]) as $pkgs
  | ([ $pkgs[].SPDXID ] + ["SPDXRef-DOCUMENT"]) as $ids
  | {
      spdxVersion, dataLicense, SPDXID, name, documentNamespace, creationInfo,
      packages: $pkgs,
      relationships: [ .relationships[]
        | select((.spdxElementId as $a | $ids | index($a))
             and (.relatedSpdxElement as $b | $ids | index($b))) ]
    }
' full-sbom.spdx.json > creator-sbom.spdx.json
```

Result: SPDX-2.3, 22 packages, 26 relationships (~41 KB).

## What the selection exercises

The packages are chosen to cover the cases the policy and distill must handle:

- `sbom` -- the SPDX document-root package (no version, no purl); must be
  excluded by `sbom-facts.jq` via its `DESCRIBES`-from-`SPDXRef-DOCUMENT` edge.
- `.ruby-rundeps`, `minitest-ci`, `ruby`, `selenium-manager` -- real packages
  with no license (`licenseConcluded` and `licenseDeclared` both `NOASSERTION`).
- `selenium-manager` -- also has `versionInfo: UNKNOWN` and no purl.
- `bash` -- `GPL-3.0-or-later`; `gmp` -- `(LGPL-3.0-or-later OR GPL-2.0-or-later)`;
  `dmesg`/`mount` -- long `... AND GPL-... AND ...` compound expressions.
- `abbrev` -- `BSD-2-Clause AND Ruby` (a package whose real license is only in
  `licenseDeclared`, exercising the distill fallback).
- `addressable`, `ansi`, `libuuid`, `hexdump`, `skalibs-libs`, `agetty`,
  `alpine-keys`, `json`, `libncursesw`, `zlib` -- normal packages with a single
  clean license, a version, and a purl.
